_about: Config for universal_civ5_pipeline.py — the ART / DDS stage of a Civilization V
Brave New World custom-civ build for the Mississippian civilization.
This script builds the icon atlases, alpha/team icons, unit flags, Dawn of Man image,
map image, and leader scene, then writes IconTextureAtlas XML, a DDS manifest, a QA
report, and per-asset previews.
It does NOT generate Civilization.sql / Leader.sql / Trait.sql / Text.sql / Colors.sql
/ the .modinfo, etc. Those gameplay tables belong to the separate SQL/text/.modinfo
stage of the builder. The full intended civ is documented for that stage under the
_civ_design_reference key below, which this art pipeline ignores.
JSON has no comment syntax, so all explanatory text lives in _-prefixed keys that the
pipeline does not read.
Paths are relative to the directory you run the script from. Put the attached Mississippian
PNG files at the listed Input/MISSISSIPPIAN_CUSTOM/ paths. Filenames must NOT carry trailing numeric suffixes
like _1 or _256.
Run:
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config MISSISSIPPIAN_CUSTOM.dds.json --check-only
python universal_civ5_pipeline.py --config MISSISSIPPIAN_CUSTOM.dds.json
Atlas layout produced by this build (fixed in code, not set here):
Normal icon atlas: 4x4 grid (16 cells); sizes 256,128,80,64,45,32 (256 atlas = 1024x1024).
Unit flag atlas:   8x8 grid, 32px cells (256x256).
Leader scene:      1280x720 because this source scene is widescreen.
Dawn of Man:       1024x768.
The IconTextureAtlas XML it emits already matches this 4x4 / 16-cell layout.
_source_art_naming: Unique unit icon     -> units[].source           -> ChunkeyWarrior_Icon.png / RiverRaider_Icon.png
Unique unit flag     -> flags[].source           -> ChunkeyWarrior_UnitFlag_black.png / RiverRaider_UnitFlag_black.png
Unique building icon -> buildings[].source       -> CahokiaPlaza_Icon.png / PlatformMound_Icon.png
Dawn of Man image    -> dawn_image               -> DawnOfMan_Mississippian.png
_icon_fit_policy_note: auto = cover full-bleed/disc/photo art to fill the circle, contain glyph art at vanilla occupancy. Use 'contain' to force strict occupancy for everything, 'cover' to always fill.

_note: NOT read by the art pipeline. This documents the Mississippian art set so the
separate SQL/text/.modinfo stage can consume the same names. Replacement targets,
trait effects, yields, flavors, and gameplay SQL MUST be verified against the
vanilla BNW database before implementation. Anything unmapped belongs in

mod.name: Mississippian Civilization (Custom)
mod.teaser: Adds the Mississippian civilization under the Great Sun.
mod.description: A custom Brave New World civilization: the Mississippian civilization, led by the Great Sun, with Mississippian-themed units, buildings, and support/promotion art.

civilization.starting_bias_note: Set CivilizationType start bias only if supported by the verified DB schema.

leader._flavor_note: Use only FlavorTypes that exist in the vanilla DB. Values are placeholders 1-10.

trait.description_text: PLACEHOLDER trait effect — map to verified Trait columns or Lua-required implementation later.

candidate_effects.description: Placeholder: bonuses connected to mounds, river settlement, or ceremonial centers.
candidate_effects.mapping_status: VERIFY — art config cannot validate gameplay tables.

unique_units._replacement_note: Confirm the exact UnitType and UnitClass in the vanilla BNW DB before generating SQL.
Confirm the exact UnitType and UnitClass in the vanilla BNW DB before generating SQL.

unique_buildings._replacement_note: Confirm the exact BuildingType and BuildingClass in the vanilla BNW DB before generating SQL.
Confirm the exact BuildingType and BuildingClass in the vanilla BNW DB before generating SQL.

_civ_design_reference._city_names_note: 16 placeholder site/city names; review and edit before gameplay release.
_civ_design_reference._spy_names_note: 10 placeholder names/titles; review and edit before gameplay release.

colors._format_note: Civ V Colors are stored as 0.0-1.0 RGBA floats in the Colors table. Values below are placeholders chosen to match the source art palette.
